item.title: Pitch in at Hermann Park
item.description:
Wednesday, July 1, 2026 at 8:30 AM

Location: Lake Plaza

Give the Park you love a little extra care, and help us get ready for the world! Join us for “Pitch in at Hermann Park,” a hands-on volunteer series where community members of all ages come together to help care for Houston’s most cherished green spaces. We’re proud to work alongside FWC26Houston and Harris County–Houston Sports Authority in the Impact Houston 26 efforts to elevate the local environmental action on a global stage and build a lasting, positive legacy for our city. You’ll work alongside Conservancy staff on simple but meaningful projects like mulching, planting, and litter cleanup — the kind of small efforts that make a big impact for visitors, wildlife, and the health of the Park. This volunteer session will be in the Heart of the Park (6104 Hermann Park Drive, Houston Texas 77030). Volunteers will meet at the Pedal Boat Kiosk. Enter through Lot G (near the Zoo) or Lot E and park in Lot F.  Walk up the concrete sidewalk to the Pedal Boat Kiosk, and we will be watching for your group. No experience is needed — just comfortable clothes, closed-toe shoes, and a willingness to help. We’ll provide the tools, guidance, and a great way to spend your morning outdoors while meeting new people and giving back to the community. Whether you come with friends, family, coworkers, or on your own, your time truly matters here. Help us welcome the world to Hermann Park! SIGN UP HERE. Weather:   Volunteer projects are rain or shine, unless there is severe weather. Cancellations due to severe weather will be sent via text/email at least 1 hour before your shift. Use your best judgment when traveling in inclement weather. Questions? Email volunteer@hermannpark.org or call 713-524-5876. Remember, we are often out in the field, so email is the preferred method of getting in touch!

From Hermann Park Conservancy

item.title: Free Watch Party: USA vs Bosnia & Herzegovina
item.description:
Wednesday, July 1, 2026 at 5:30 PM

The Round of 32 rolls on, and the field keeps getting smaller. Every pass, every save, every goal carries the weight of a nation tonight. Come celebrate the beautiful game with us as more World Cup dreams are made and broken. Festival gates open at 5:30 p.m. come early an enjoy the sounds of DJ Athenz. Kickoff is at 7:00 p.m. Admission is free! Food and Beverage Concessions: Enjoy a variety of festival food fare, beer and wine, and non-alcoholic beverages from Coca-Cola. Visit Smalls Sliders for burgers, fries, and shakes and cool down with Frios Gourmet Pops. Stop by Hearsay’s festival tent for a cold beer or beer bucket or grab a michelada, wine, or beer from Gaspacho’s festival tent. Non-alcoholic drinks from Coca-Cola as well as pre-packaged snacks can be purchased at the Community Partner Concessions Booth, proceeds benefiting Levy Park and the event’s community partners. Community Partner: We are proud to be joined by our Community Partner, Dynamo & Dash Charities, for this event! By leveraging the passion of players, staff, and fans, Dynamo & Dash Charities provides resources and education to positively impact lives in the community. Stop by the Community Partner Booth to support their amazing work and learn more about their initiatives. All food & beverage proceeds from the Community Partner booth will support Dynamo & Dash Charities and Levy Park. Face Painting & Henna Art: Get into the tournament spirit with face and body art from Gio’s Art Kreations and Henna Station & Glitter Bar Merchandise: Event merchandise celebrating Houston, Levy Park, and the global soccer tournament will also be available for purchase. Art Installations and Photo Opportunities: Commemorate the experience with a photo tour of the park, including life-sized soccer-themed photo backdrops and a tournament bracket. Visit our soccer ball music box and turn the crank for festive sounds. Festival Guidelines: VISITORS MUST COMPLY WITH DIRECTIONS FROM PARK PERSONNEL, OR THEY WILL BE ASKED TO LEAVE THE PARK. PARENTS ARE RESPONSIBLE FOR THEIR CHILDREN’S SAFETY IN THE PARK. CHAIRS AND SEATING: No outside furniture. Blankets may be brought into the Festival site for the designated blanket seating area. All other furniture is available on a first-come, first-served basis. STROLLERS & WAGONS: Oversized strollers and wagons may need to be parked in a designated area during the festival. All visitors must comply with park personnel regarding stroller and wagon parking. FOOD AND BEVERAGES: Guests are permitted to enter with one factory-sealed bottle of water. Outside food and drinks are prohibited (exceptions will be made for baby food and necessary medical dietary food items). A variety of food, non-alcoholic drinks, beer, and wine will be available for purchase from Festival concessionaires. RE-ENTRY: If you leave the Festival site, you will be re-screened before re-entry. THE FOLLOWING ARE PROHIBITED: Smoking or Vaping Umbrellas Drug Use Kites and Drones Recreational Motorized Vehicles Guns, knives, any weaponry Soliciting Bikes, scooters, rollerblades, and roller skates

From Levy Park Houston

item.title: Ion Networking with Cup of Joey
item.description:
Friday, July 3, 2026 at 8:30 AM

Location: Ion – Lobby, 4201 Main St., Houston, 77002, United States

What is the purpose of Cup of Joey? It is to create a connected culture of coffee shop-open networking across our region. Every Friday morning, you’ll have the opportunity to meet new and expand your current networks, talk about tech, inspire innovation, and create community. So, what is the difference between a cup of joe and a Cup of Joe-Y? It is the WHY. It’s not what you do. It is why you do it! At the Ion, these are questions that we want to explore with the tech and innovation community. So, show up, be friendly, and make new connections. Know your why, and we will get to the future together. The future is only a cup away. Register Here: https://www.cupofjoey.org/

From Ion District

item.title: Houston TCG Con IX
item.description:
Friday, July 3, 2026 at 10 AM

Houston TCG Con IX — Your Favorite TCG Weekend in Texas Three days. 100+ vendors. Every game that matters. Houston TCG Con IX is back — and the Texas TCG community keeps showing up stronger every round! Pokémon. Yu-Gi-Oh!. Magic: The Gathering. One Piece. Lorcana. Riftbound. Dragon Ball Super. Digimon. Flesh and Blood. If it’s cardboard worth chasing, it’s on this floor.   📅 Date: Friday–Sunday, July 3–5, 2026 ⏰ Time: Friday: 3PM – 10PM | Saturday: 10AM – 6PM | Sunday: 10AM – 5PM 📍 Location: 9700 Bissonnet St, Unit 1500, Houston, TX 77036 🎟️ FREE Entry + FREE Parking   🔥 Event Highlights 🔥 🃏 100+ TCG Vendors — Three Days of Cardboard Shop from 100+ vendors traveling from across Texas and beyond. Find singles, sealed product, booster boxes, graded slabs, Japanese imports, and rare inserts across every major title — Pokémon, MTG, Yu-Gi-Oh!, One Piece, Lorcana, Riftbound, Dragon Ball Super, Digimon, Flesh and Blood, and more. Come with a wantlist. Leave with something you weren’t expecting.   🤝 The Trade Floor Is Open Bring your binders, your bulk, your trade bait, and your spec pulls. Houston TCG Con is a trade-friendly floor — deals happen naturally here because the people at the tables are collectors first. Three days means three days of opportunities to find the trade that makes your collection.   💸 Deals Across Every Budget From budget bulk and bargain boxes to high-end slabs and competitive singles — the floor has range. Whether you’re spec buying, PC hunting, or just looking to flip smart, TCG Con IX has the inventory to make it happen.   💼 Vendor Space Available Table space is open for TCG and collectibles vendors. Come connect with the Texas TCG community. 👉 Apply here: https://forms.gle/KFdA1u3pKRAvNtzJ9   🏨 Traveling to Houston? Lock In Your Rate We’ve secured a special hotel rate exclusively for TCG Con IX attendees. Book your stay and make it a full weekend. 👉 Book here   Three days. One floor. The best TCG weekend in Texas. Houston TCG Con IX — July 3–5.

From CCS Houston

item.title: Freedom Over Texas 2026
item.description:
Saturday, July 4, 2026 at 3 PM

Location: Eleanor Tinsley Park, 18-3600 Allen Pkwy, Houston, TX, 77019, United States

Friday, July 4, 3 pm to 10 pmEleanor Tinsley Park on Buffalo Bayou The most anticipated summer fun in the City of Houston, celebrating its 39th year, the annual Freedom Over Texas returns on Saturday, July 4 at Eleanor Tinsley and Sam Houston Parks. From 3 pm – 10 pm, this extraordinary, fun-filled celebration highlights the traditions of Independence Day, as well as honoring the nation’s 250-year anniversary. As an added bonus Houston Food Bank will receive a percentage of concessions as a donation (up to $10,000), helping to fill an extraordinary demand in our regional community. The Freedom Stage will be showcasing Los Lonely Boys to kick off the night, followed by Collective Soul opening for our headliner Keith Urban, who is sure to entertain music fans through the evening. For friends, families, and fans in town for soccer, there is something for everyone to enjoy, including Fútbol Park, Liberty Park, the Kids Zone, Budweiser Zone, and Dr. Pepper’s Flavor Zone. All of it leads up to a spectacularly choreographed, Texas-sized fireworks finale. Freedom Over Texas draws an average crowd of 35,000 attendees each year and is broadcast live on ABC KTRK Channel 13 across Houston, with outreach to television affiliates statewide to simulcast portions of the event. Learn More and Purchase Tickets

From Houston Food Bank

item.title: Red, White & Bloom
item.description:
Saturday, July 4, 2026 at 8 PM

Location: McGovern Centennial Gardens, 1500 Hermann Drive, Houston, TX, 77004, United States

Celebrate the Fourth of July surrounded by beautiful blooms, festive fun, and one of Houston’s most beloved fireworks displays! Join us for “Red, White & Bloom” on Saturday, July 4, from 8 – 11 p.m. in the McGovern Centennial Gardens for a fantastic summer evening. This year is extra special as we mark America’s 250th birthday with a celebration packed with birthday party vibes! Enjoy free treats, family-friendly activities, music, lawn games, and more before the night wraps up with a spectacular view of the fireworks show from Miller Outdoor Theatre. Best of all, Red, White & Bloom is FREE and open to the public! Bring a blanket, gather your friends and family, and stroll through the vibrant garden spaces before settling in for the show. Spend Independence Day in this iconic spot, under the stars—a perfect setting for summer memories! The fireworks show at Miller Outdoor Theatre is set to start at approximately 9:45 p.m. The complimentary food items are while supplies last. Additional food vendors will be offering items for purchase by Miller Outdoor Theatre.

From Hermann Park Conservancy

Event details

item.title: Red, White & Cruise (SOLD OUT!)
item.description:
Saturday, July 4, 2026 at 8:30 PM

Location: Lake Plaza

THIS EVENT HAS SOLD OUT! IF YOU WOULD LIKE TO BE PLACED ON A WAITLIST IF ANY SPOTS OPEN UP, PLEASE EMAIL INFO@HERMANNPARK.ORG. Join us for Red, White & Cruise where you can watch Miller Outdoor Theatre’s fireworks show light up the sky from the peaceful waters of Hermann Park, with a special pedal boat rental from 8:30–10:30 p.m. on Saturday, July 4! As we celebrate 250 years of American independence, gather your crew and watch the sky come alive with fireworks while cruising across the lake in your own pedal boat under the stars. Saturday, July 4 8:30 – 10:30 p.m.  🎟️ $150 per boat (up to 4 people) includes: 🕘 Boat rental up to 2 hours 🍿 Drinks & snacks 🎊 Festive accessories 🅿️ Reserved parking in Lot I near Lake Plaza ⛵ Board anytime between 8:30–9:30 p.m. and enjoy the show from the water (fireworks begin at approximately 9:45 p.m., boats must return once fireworks finish) BOOK YOUR BOAT HERE!  🚨 Important Info: One rider per boat must be 18+ All riders 18+ must sign a digital waiver before boarding At least one rider must be 18+, with a physical government ID, and sign a waiver before boarding Tickets are non-refundable Event is rain or shine per Miller Outdoor Theatre’s plan In the event of lightning, pedal boats will close, and refunds will be issued  The purchase of a ticket includes an up-to 2-hour pedal boat ride for up to 4 people to enjoy the sight of Miller Outdoor Theatre’s fireworks show, drinks, snacks, festive treats, and a reserved parking spot in Lot I near Lott Hall by Lake Plaza (starting at 8 p.m.). Only one ticket purchase of $150 is needed for a 4-person boat (i.e. it is the cost per boat, not per rider).  A reminder that at least one rider per boat must be 18 years or older.  All riders 18 years or older must sign a digital waiver before boarding. Tickets are non-refundable. We are following Miller Outdoor Theatre’s plan for their event and fireworks show which is rain or shine. If there is lightning in the area, we will close the pedal boats and ticket holders will receive a refund.  Please arrive early to allow time to park in Lot I as roads in the Park will be closed by HPD and closures are subject to change based on their purview.  

From Hermann Park Conservancy

item.title: Free Watch Party: Round of 16 (W81 vs W82)
item.description:
Monday, July 6, 2026 at 5:30 PM

The first knock out round is over and the field has been cut in half. Every match from here is win or go home, and tonight someone’s World Cup journey ends. The Round of 16 is knockout soccer at its most raw and thrilling. Be here for it! Festival gates open at 5:30 p.m. to the sounds of DJ Athenz. Kickoff is at 7:00 p.m. Admission is free! Food and Beverage Concessions: Enjoy a variety of festival food fare, beer and wine, and non-alcoholic beverages from Coca-Cola. Visit Smalls Sliders for burgers, fries, and shakes and cool down with Frios Gourmet Pops. Stop by Hearsay’s festival tent for a cold beer or beer bucket or grab a michelada, wine, or beer from Gaspacho’s festival tent. Non-alcoholic drinks from Coca-Cola as well as pre-packaged snacks can be purchased at the Community Partner Concessions Booth, proceeds benefiting Levy Park and the event’s community partners. Community Partners: Supporting this event is the Japan-America Society of Houston. The Japan-America Society of Houston (JASH) is the leading organization that connects Houston’s diverse community, local businesses, and individuals to advance the mutual interest of American and Japanese peoples. JASH programs in language and education, arts and culture, and business initiatives and networking provide opportunities for collaboration and important people-to-people exchange. JASH hosts a monthly program at Levy Park: Japan Junction, as well as seasonal programming throughout the year. All proceeds from the Community Partner Concessions booth will support JASH and Levy Park. Face Painting & Henna Art: Get into the tournament spirit with face and body art from Gio’s Art Kreations and Henna Station & Glitter Bar Merchandise: Event merchandise celebrating Houston, Levy Park, and the global soccer tournament will also be available for purchase. Art Installations and Photo Opportunities: Commemorate the experience with a photo tour of the park, including life-sized soccer-themed photo backdrops and a tournament bracket. Visit our soccer ball music box and turn the crank for festive sounds. Festival Guidelines: VISITORS MUST COMPLY WITH DIRECTIONS FROM PARK PERSONNEL, OR THEY WILL BE ASKED TO LEAVE THE PARK. PARENTS ARE RESPONSIBLE FOR THEIR CHILDREN’S SAFETY IN THE PARK. CHAIRS AND SEATING: No outside furniture. Blankets may be brought into the Festival site for the designated blanket seating area. All other furniture is available on a first-come, first-served basis. STROLLERS & WAGONS: Oversized strollers and wagons may need to be parked in a designated area during the festival. All visitors must comply with park personnel regarding stroller and wagon parking. FOOD AND BEVERAGES: Guests are permitted to enter with one factory-sealed bottle of water. Outside food and drinks are prohibited (exceptions will be made for baby food and necessary medical dietary food items). A variety of food, non-alcoholic drinks, beer, and wine will be available for purchase from Festival concessionaires. RE-ENTRY: If you leave the Festival site, you will be re-screened before re-entry. THE FOLLOWING ARE PROHIBITED: Smoking or Vaping Umbrellas Drug Use Kites and Drones Recreational Motorized Vehicles Guns, knives, any weaponry Soliciting Bikes, scooters, rollerblades, and roller skates

From Levy Park Houston

item.title: How Oil and Gas Professionals are Building Wealth Smarter
item.description:
Tuesday, July 7, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: Ion, 4201 Main Street, Houston, TX, 77002, United States

Register here: https://www.eventbrite.com/e/how-oil-and-gas-professionals-are-building-wealth-smarter-tickets-1992002445939?aff=oddtdtcreator&keep_tld=true Join us for an engaging presentation designed for O&G professionals who want to cut through the confusion, and take well informed steps to growing and preserving their wealth. We’ll explore how thoughtful decisions around investing, taxes, and retirement planning can significantly influence your long-term financial outcomes. You’ll learn: – Practical strategies for building a clear and effective financial plan – Four key principles that contribute to a successful retirement – Tax-efficient approaches that may help you retain more of your wealth – Common investment mistakes that can erode long-term results—and how to avoid them Whether you’re early in your career or approaching retirement, this session will help you make more informed financial decisions and gain greater confidence in your path forward.

From Ion District

Event details

item.title: What AI, Cybersecurity, and Tequila Have in Common.
item.description:
Tuesday, July 7, 2026 at 5 PM

Location: Ion, 4201 Main Street, Houston, TX, 77002, United States

Register here: https://luma.com/0rfy7te0 Most companies treat AI like a shortcut to growth: it delivers immediate results, but the long-term operational fallout is brutal. ​Everyone is caught up in the rush, plugging automated tools into critical infrastructure without checking the foundation. But speed without security isn’t progress, it’s an open invitation for cyber threats. It’s time to face the structural reality. ​Join Blue People and Alpfa Houston for an unfiltered session with Reynaldo Gonzalez, an industry veteran with 18+ years of experience securing critical infrastructure. ​The Realities We’re Breaking Down: ​AI doesn’t change the value of knowledge; it changes the value of curiosity. The ultimate security edge belongs to leaders who know how to ask the right questions, not just trust the output. ​Your biggest competitive advantage is becoming more human, not less. In a world of automated systems, human judgment and sharp intuition remain the only truly un-hackable firewalls left. ​AI is the greatest operational amplifier ever created. It scales your wins, but it weaponizes your internal flaws. If your baseline security is weak, AI just helps you fail faster and at scale. ​The single biggest risk today isn’t AI, it’s human complacency. The danger isn’t rogue technology or advanced malware; it’s our own blind trust in automated workflows. ​Stop operating on hope. Turn cyber resilience into your unfair advantage. ​📅 July 7th, 2026 | 5:00 PM – 7:00 PM 📍 The Ion Houston Hosted by Blue People & Alpfa Houston ​👉 Ask about our parking options at the end of the event!

From Ion District

item.title: Pitch in at Hermann Park
item.description:
Wednesday, July 8, 2026 at 8:30 AM

Location: Lake Plaza

Give the Park you love a little extra care, and help us get ready for the world! Join us for “Pitch in at Hermann Park,” a hands-on volunteer series where community members of all ages come together to help care for Houston’s most cherished green spaces. We’re proud to work alongside FWC26Houston and Harris County–Houston Sports Authority in the Impact Houston 26 efforts to elevate the local environmental action on a global stage and build a lasting, positive legacy for our city. You’ll work alongside Conservancy staff on simple but meaningful projects like mulching, planting, and litter cleanup — the kind of small efforts that make a big impact for visitors, wildlife, and the health of the Park. This volunteer session will be in the Heart of the Park (6104 Hermann Park Drive, Houston Texas 77030). Volunteers will meet at the Pedal Boat Kiosk. Enter through Lot G (near the Zoo) or Lot E and park in Lot F.  Walk up the concrete sidewalk to the Pedal Boat Kiosk, and we will be watching for your group. No experience is needed — just comfortable clothes, closed-toe shoes, and a willingness to help. We’ll provide the tools, guidance, and a great way to spend your morning outdoors while meeting new people and giving back to the community. Whether you come with friends, family, coworkers, or on your own, your time truly matters here. Help us welcome the world to Hermann Park! SIGN UP HERE. Weather:   Volunteer projects are rain or shine, unless there is severe weather. Cancellations due to severe weather will be sent via text/email at least 1 hour before your shift. Use your best judgment when traveling in inclement weather. Questions? Email volunteer@hermannpark.org or call 713-524-5876. Remember, we are often out in the field, so email is the preferred method of getting in touch!

From Hermann Park Conservancy

Event details

item.title: Japan Junction
item.description:
Wednesday, July 8, 2026 at 4:30 PM

Location: Levy Park (Event Pavilion), TX

June 10: “The Tubmaker Who Flew to the Sea” and Teru Teru Bozu paper craft June in Japan is known as Tsuyu, the rainy season, when gentle rains and cloudy skies become a part of daily life. But what do you do when you are hoping for sunny weather before an important day? In Japan, people often hang Teru Teru Bozu, a traditional handmade decoration believed to bring good weather. Usually made from tissue paper or cloth, these cheerful little figures are displayed near windows or doors to wish for sunshine. Join us for a kamishibai reading of “Okeya no Ten Nobori” or “The Tubmaker Who Flew to the Sky,” a whimsical tale featuring a sleepy thunder god, Kaminari-sama, whose storms bring rainy weather. After the story, make your own Teru Teru Bozu out of craft paper to chase away the rain and Kaminari-sama and invite sunny skies!

From Levy Park Houston

item.title: Buying and Selling Businesses
item.description:
Wednesday, July 8, 2026 at 5:30 PM

Location: Ion – Conference Room 029 – 030, 4201 Main St, Houston, TX, 77002, United States

Join Lenny Saizan, owner of Murphy Business & Financial – Southwest Houston, Managing Director of Urban Capital Network, and creator of the Legacy Maximizer exit strategy, for a practical, real-world discussion on selling and buying businesses. Selling or acquiring a business is one of the most significant financial decisions most entrepreneurs will ever make, yet many owners and buyers go through the process for the first time without a clear roadmap. This session breaks down the transaction process from both sides of the table and highlights how thoughtful planning, proper valuation, and coordinated advice can materially impact outcomes. For business owners, the discussion centers on how to maximize enterprise value, prepare for a future exit, and align business decisions with personal wealth, tax, and legacy goals. For buyers, the session explores entrepreneurship through acquisition, including how buying an existing business can reduce risk, generate immediate cash flow, and serve as a growth strategy for current operators. Drawing on his experience as a business broker, investment advisor, and financial professional, Lenny connects the technical aspects of M&A with broader exit planning and wealth strategy considerations, helping attendees understand not just how transactions work, but how they fit into a bigger financial picture. Topics include: Current market conditions and buyer demand When to prepare to sell or buy Key drivers of business value How businesses are valued in the real world Recasting and normalizing financial statements Overview of the sales and acquisition process The role of advisors and the deal team Tax, insurance, and planning considerations before and after a transaction This session is ideal for business owners considering a sale now or in the future, prospective buyers exploring acquisition, and professionals who support small business owners through growth and transition. Presenter Lenny Saizan is the owner and lead broker of Murphy Business & Financial – Southwest Houston, where he advises business owners on exit planning, value maximization, and business sales, and supports buyers pursuing acquisitions and franchises. He is also the co-founder and Managing Director of Urban Capital Network, an investment firm focused on expanding access to private investment opportunities. Earlier in his career, Lenny spent two decades in engineering, automation, and oil and gas, leading teams across operational controls, financial controls, and audit. He holds a Bachelor of Science in Electrical Engineering from Louisiana State University and an MBA from Tulane University. His credentials include Certified Mergers & Acquisition Professional, Six Sigma Green Belt, and Project Management Professional. In 2025, he was recognized as Deal Maker of The Year at Houston Dealmakers conference.  

From Ion District

item.title: Upstream: Digital Tech Meetup at Second Draught
item.description:
Thursday, July 9, 2026 at 5:30 PM

Location: Second Draught, 4201 Main St. Suite 130, Houston, TX, 77002, United States

Register here: https://luma.com/timbergrove Upstream: Digital Tech Meetup – Monthly Social 🍻 No stage. No slides. No panels. Just upstream operators, engineers, founders, and digital leaders getting together over beer and pizza. This is the informal side of the Upstream Digital Tech community. A casual, vendor-light happy hour for people navigating digital change in oil and gas and wanting real conversations. It’s not a presentation. It’s not a pitch night. It’s a room full of people who are actually doing the work. A place to: • Meet others working at the intersection of upstream and technology • Trade field stories, lessons learned, and hard truths • Talk AI, IoT, data, automation, and deployment realities • Build real relationships without a formal agenda Whether you’re deploying technology, evaluating it, building it, or leading teams through it, you’ll fit right in. Come as you are. Bring a friend. Stay as long as you like. 📍 Second Draught @ The Ion District, Midtown Houston 🗓️ Second Thursday of every month | ⏰ 5:30 PM 👉 Reserve your spot. Space is limited.

item.title: Welcome to the Ion: An Introduction and Tour
item.description:
Friday, July 10, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Ion, 4201 Main Street, Houston, TX, 77002, United States

Join the Ion’s Mentor in Residence, Wade Pinder, for an engaging tour and introduction to the Ion, Houston’s dynamic hub for innovation and entrepreneurship, powered by Rice University. This event is designed for entrepreneurs, students, professionals, investors, community leaders, and anyone curious about what the Ion has to offer. For those new to Houston and eager to connect with the local innovation ecosystem, this event provides the perfect opportunity to get started. The tour will begin in the lobby at 10:00 AM and conclude at 11:00 AM. During this time, participants will learn about the Ion’s resources, events, and facilities. Highlights of the tour include the technology showcases, coworking spaces, public areas, and the on-site restaurants and bars. About Wade Pinder Wade Pinder is a dedicated advocate for Houston’s innovation community, serving as the Ion’s Mentor in Residence. With a wealth of experience in product management, community building, and fostering entrepreneurial growth, Wade offers invaluable insights into how the Ion serves as a launchpad for ideas and innovation. The Vision of the Ion Powered by Rice University, the Ion is the epicenter of the 16-acre innovation district in the heart of Houston’s midtown. Committed to being the home for Houston’s innovation community, the Ion District propels the transformative culture of people, places, ideas, and experiences that build a better quality of life for all of Houston. The Ion is a beautifully restored building at 4201 Main Street. It connects Houston’s talent, academic, corporate, and entrepreneurial communities in a collaborative environment designed to inspire and grow ideas. The Ion serves as a space where serendipitous collisions lead to meaningful connections and opportunities. What to Expect Coworking Spaces: Explore flexible work areas tailored for collaboration and creativity. Public Spaces: Discover spaces designed for networking, inspiration, and community events. Restaurants and Bars: Experience the on-site dining options that make the Ion a lively hub. Through its programs and partnerships, the Ion empowers individuals and organizations to build, solve, and innovate. From prototyping to pitching, accelerating to sustaining, the Ion is Houston’s headquarters for turning ideas into reality. Whether you have a promising product, an intriguing technology, a budding business idea, or are simply curious about the Ion and its offerings, this tour will provide valuable insights into how the Ion can help connect your “What-ifs” with “What now.” Don’t miss this opportunity to explore and be inspired by Houston’s innovation ecosystem.

From Ion District

item.title: Sunset Bingo at Levy Park
item.description:
Friday, July 10, 2026 at 6 PM

Bring your crew the second Friday of each month for a fun evening where you can take home fantastic prizes. Set to the backdrop of soulful tunes from DJ Del Sur, Sunset Bingo is a great way to unwind and support Levy Park. All event proceeds directly support the park’s operations and programming. Please note: While children (and pups) can play, an adult (18+) must be present to claim a prize. $10 for 8 games; $1 daubers are available for purchase, or BYODauber (bring your own dauber). CASH ONLY Event Schedule: 6 p.m. – Board Sales Begin 7 p.m. – Games Begin Food & Beverage Concessions: Enjoy convenient counter service with easy-to-eat, handheld favorites like tortas, flautas, hot dogs, and tacos at Gaspachos Mexican Bites—plus special treats for your four-legged friends! Hearsay is also serving up its eclectic cuisine, refreshing cocktails, and treats for your pup. Kind Ice Cream offers a 10% discount during games, allowing Bingo participants to enjoy its irresistible selection of cakes and ice cream treats. Outside alcohol is not permitted but may be purchased from park concessionaires. No outside furniture is allowed. Park furniture will be available, or guests may bring a blanket for lawn seating. Parking Parking is available adjacent to the park and along Eastside Street and Wakeforest Avenue. Additional public parking (parking rate applies) is available in the Kirby Grove parking garage located on the corner of Richmond and Wakeforest. Please note: Prizes and sponsors may vary by event. If you would like to donate an in-kind prize or sponsor Sunset Bingo, please contact us at info@levyparkhouston.org.

item.title: Artist Alley Houston III
item.description:
Saturday, July 11, 2026 at 10 AM

Artist Alley Houston III — 100+ Local Creators. All Original. All Handmade. Print hunters, charm collectors, plushie lovers, and fandom art fanatics — Artist Alley Houston III is your weekend. Two days, 100+ local and regional creators, and zero mass-produced merch. Every piece on every table was made by the person standing behind it.   📅 Date: Saturday–Sunday, July 11–12, 2026 ⏰ Time: Saturday: 10AM – 6PM | Sunday: 10AM – 5PM 📍 Location: 9700 Bissonnet St, Unit 1500, Houston, TX 77036 🎟️ FREE Entry + FREE Parking   🔥 Event Highlights 🔥 🎨 100+ Artists and Makers — All Fandoms, All Styles Explore a curated marketplace packed with anime fan art, original illustrations, stickers, enamel pins, art prints, zines, plushies, handcrafted charms, crochet creations, accessories, fantasy artwork, and pop-culture-inspired pieces — all original, all handmade, no AI-generated art. Whether you’re shopping for your wall, your ita bag, your shelf, or just yourself, this floor delivers. Come ready to discover your next favorite Houston creator.   ✨ Exclusive Drops and Event Debuts Many artists are launching event-only merch, limited bundles, brand-new releases, and first-time debuts created specifically for Artist Alley Houston III. You won’t find these anywhere else — online or in stores. Bring a tote bag. You’re going to need it.   💬 Meet the Makers Talk with the artists. Hear the stories behind the work. Commission something made just for you. This is not just a shopping floor — it’s where Houston’s creative community shows up in person. Fellow creators, longtime collectors, and first-time visitors all belong here.   🎁 FREE Artist Raffle Win original art, stickers, keychains, and handmade goods donated by our featured artists. No purchase necessary — just stop by, sign up, and you’re in. Every ticket is a chance to go home with something one-of-a-kind.   💌 Join the Artist Lineup Table space is open for local artists and makers. Come build with the Houston creative community. 👉 Apply here   🏨 Traveling to Houston? Lock In Your Rate We’ve secured a special hotel rate exclusively for Artist Alley Houston III attendees. Book your stay and make it a full weekend. 👉 Book here   Two days. 100+ creators. Art made with love, sold in person. Artist Alley Houston III — July 11 & 12.

item.title: Introduction to Teaching the Holocaust
item.description:
Tuesday, July 14, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: Holocaust Museum Houston Classroom

Join Holocaust Museum Houston for a one day workshop designed to help educators to feel comfortable teaching about the Holocaust. The day will include a tour of the museum’s Holocaust Gallery, a talk from a Holocaust survivor or descendant, best practices for teaching about the Holocaust, an overview of some of the free resources available, and more.  Breakfast and lunch will be provided. Teachers will earn 6 CPE hours and 6 GT update hours. Teachers from the 6th-12th grade, in all subject areas, are encouraged to participate in this FREE workshop. Sign up through the registration link below. If you have any questions about the workshop, contact us as education@hmh.org.    Interested in a more in-depth exploration of the Holocaust? We also offer the Max M. Kaplan Summer Institute for Educators, which includes pedagogical and content sessions from scholars and museum educators. You can find more information and apply here: https://hmh.org/education/professional-development/max-m-kaplan-summer-institute/. Register Here

From Holocaust Museum Houston

Event details

item.title: Why Networking Isn’t Turning Into Deals — And What To Do Instead
item.description:
Tuesday, July 14, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: Ion, 4201 Main Street, Houston, TX, 77002, United States

Register here: https://www.eventbrite.com/e/1991195256614?aff=oddtdtcreator Networking is one of the most common business development activities in oil & gas, energy, and industrial sectors. But for many companies, networking is not consistently turning into real opportunities. You’re attending events. You’re meeting people. You’re having conversations. You’re building relationships. But the pipeline does not always reflect it. This session is designed to break down why networking often fails to convert into deals—and what companies can do differently to turn conversations into qualified opportunities and real business development momentum. Because networking alone is not a strategy. A strong relationship can open the door, but without structure, follow-up, and clear movement, opportunities often stall before they ever become real pipeline. What You’ll Learn Why networking alone does not create predictable growth The difference between visibility, conversations, and actual pipeline Where most companies lose momentum after an event or introduction How to follow up in a way that creates real opportunities How to turn relationships into qualified conversations and deal flow What a simple system looks like for moving from connection to opportunity Who Should Attend Oil & gas operators and service companies Energy and industrial organizations Executives, business development leaders, and sales teams Founders and operators responsible for growth Companies that attend events, conferences, and industry gatherings but want better conversion from those conversations Event Format 30–40 minute presentation 15–20 minute discussion and Q&A Optional networking following the session About the Speaker Jada Powell is the Founder of Powell Consulting Group, where she works with oil & gas, energy, and industrial companies to build structured, repeatable business development systems that drive consistent pipeline and revenue growth. Her work focuses on helping mid-market companies move beyond relationship-driven growth into more scalable, measurable commercial strategies. This session is part of The Pipeline Series: How Oil & Gas Companies Actually Grow Revenue, a monthly in-person series at The Ion focused on helping oil & gas, energy, and industrial companies create more consistent pipeline, stronger follow-up, and better conversion from conversations to revenue.

From Ion District

item.title: Educating Traumatized Teenagers: The Extraordinary Story of a Swedish International School
item.description:
Thursday, July 16, 2026 at 6:30 PM

After World War II, Sweden absorbed and provided medical treatment for thousands of sick survivors. What to do about those who were teenagers, who had missed years of study on account of the war?  In September 1945, the Swedish government issued an order for schooling to be offered to those under the age of 18. In this presentation, Dr. Bernice Lerner will describe the complex process of recruiting wary adolescents, the challenges of teaching homeless, orphaned, and “environmentally damaged” teenagers, and instructors’ goals and methods at the most experimental of sites—the “Internat” school her mother attended. Dr. Bernice Lerner is the author of All the Horrors of War: A Jewish Girl, a British Doctor, and the Liberation of Bergen-Belsen, and other writings on the Holocaust and on virtue ethics. She is the former dean of adult learning at Hebrew College, former lecturer on the Holocaust at Boston University, and a senior scholar at Boston University’s Center for Character and Social Responsibility. Gerald S. Kaplan Endowed Lecture.

From Holocaust Museum Houston

item.title: Summer Film: Hill “Halfon” Doesn’t Answer
item.description:
Thursday, July 16, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: Kaplan Theatre @ the J, 5601 S. Braeswood, Houston, TX, 77096

Hill “Halfon” Doesn’t Answer Directed by Asi Dayan (1976) Comedy | 92 Minutes | Hebrew with English subtitles Join us for the celebration of the 50th anniversary of the legendary film which became the greatest cult film in the history of Israeli cinema. In this satirical cult-classic, a reserve unit in the Israel-controlled Sinai is less than five miles from the Egyptian front lines. However, the Israelis are hardly concerned with the Egyptians, as half of the Reserve-niks are just happy to get away from their homes and essentially go camping in uniform, while the other half goes crazy in hilarious fashion.

item.title: Rice Tech Transfer Office Hours
item.description:
Friday, July 17, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Ion, 4201 Main Street, Houston, TX, 77002, United States

Have an idea, startup, or company looking for innovative technology? Meet with the Rice University Office of Technology Transfer during office hours at The Ion to explore how university research can power your next breakthrough. Our team works with startups, entrepreneurs, and established companies to help commercialize cutting-edge technologies developed at Rice. During these office hours, you can: Discover Available Technologies – Learn about Rice innovations available for licensing across fields like health, energy, engineering, and computing. Understand the Licensing Process – Get a clear walkthrough of how to license university technology and what is required to move forward. Explore Collaboration Opportunities – Discuss potential partnerships with Rice researchers and ways to turn research into real-world products. Whether you’re building a startup, scouting new technology, or exploring research partnerships, stop by and connect with our team. Come with a question, leave with a path to commercialization. Stay connected and hear about new technologies available for licensing: https://mailchi.mp/rice/new-email-signup-page

item.title: CCS Nerd Yard Sale III
item.description:
Saturday, July 18, 2026 at 10 AM

CCS Houston Nerd Yard Sale III — Dig In. Deal Out. Pass It On. Got shelves full of stuff that deserves a new home? Looking to score fan-loved finds at yard-sale prices? CCS Houston Nerd Yard Sale III is back for a full weekend — two days of clearing, digging, dealing, and connecting with fellow fans across every fandom and era. This isn’t a convention. It’s not a vendor hall. It’s a true community yard sale where real collectors clear their collections, real deals get made, and real conversations happen over shared fandoms.   📅 Date: Saturday–Sunday, July 18–19, 2026 ⏰ Time: Saturday: 10AM – 6PM | Sunday: 10AM – 5PM 📍 Location: 9700 Bissonnet St, Unit 1500, Houston, TX 77036 🎟️ FREE Entry + FREE Parking   🔥 Event Highlights 🔥 🧺 For Sellers — Clear Your Shelves, Make Some Cash Got boxes of fandom stuff ready to move? Duplicates, impulse buys, collections you’ve outgrown? Set up a table, meet fellow fans, and pass your collection on to someone who’ll love it just as much as you did. Perfect for collectors downsizing, families clearing closets, and anyone who needs to make space. 👉 Apply to be a seller   🔍 For Shoppers — Dig, Deal, Discover Bargain hunters, this floor is yours. Browse tables packed with fan-loved finds at yard-sale prices across every fandom and era. You never know what you’ll find — but here’s what might be waiting: 📚 Comics, manga, and books 🎮 Retro games and consoles — NES, SNES, Sega, PlayStation classics, and more 🧸 Plushies, pins, keychains, and gacha 🧩 Anime merch, figures, and Funkos 📼 VHS, vintage media, and old-school collectibles 👕 Fandom fashion and accessories 🃏 Cards, toys, and unexpected gems Prices are yard-sale friendly. Every table is different. The best finds are the ones you didn’t know you were looking for.   👨‍👩‍👧‍👦 All Fandoms. All Ages. Zero Gatekeeping. Bring the whole crew. No pressure. No gatekeeping. Just fans connecting with fans over the things they love.   🏨 Traveling to Houston? Lock In Your Rate We’ve secured a special hotel rate exclusively for Nerd Yard Sale III attendees. Book your stay and make it a full weekend. 👉 Book here   Bring your trade boxes. Bring your shopping bags. Bring your inner treasure hunter. CCS Houston Nerd Yard Sale III — July 18 & 19.

From CCS Houston

item.title: Angel Investing 101
item.description:
Tuesday, July 21, 2026 at 5:30 PM

Location: Ion – Conference Room 029 – 030, 4201 Main St, Houston, TX, 77002, United States

In a world where only the very wealthy, well connected, and major institutions have access to premium investment opportunities, which have created massive wealth for a small portion of the population, the Innovation Center for Urban Entrepreneurship team believes the key to closing income and wealth gaps globally is to democratize access to opportunities across the investment ecosystem. Join the Innovation Center for Urban Entrepreneurship team for an evening of learning, discussion and possibilities as we explore the basics of angel investing and the role it plays in bringing new products and services to market and the impact it has on wealth and job creation.. At this event, you’ll learn the key components to successfully navigate the critical-path of investing in private, early-stage companies. This session is ideal for the following investment related stakeholders: Prospective Angel Investors who want to learn more about the world of Angel Investing Investment Advisors (RIA/FA) who are looking for alternative investments for their clients Entrepreneurs who want to learn more about what Angels are looking for in their deals Entrepreneurs who want to meet/network with Angel Investors Ion District Investor Activation Program: The Houston Ion District Investor Activation Program along with Urban Capital Network is helping build an equitable climatetech industry capable of rapid deployment by addressing a fundamental challenge: the region’s lack of funding for early-stage climatetech startups, and especially for those led by underrepresented founders. This program from Greentown Labs and the Ion brings early-stage investors from Greater Houston—a region playing a pivotal role in the energy transition—into the fold, offering education on climatetech investing, channeling a pool of capital to young startups, and catalyzing a thriving climatetech investment ecosystem that prioritizes diversity, equity, and inclusion. If you would like to join the program, please fill out this form, and if you have additional questions, please contact Juliana Bernal Martinez at jbernal@greentownlabs.com.

From Ion District

item.title: NASA Tech Talks
item.description:
Thursday, July 23, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: Ion, 4201 Main Street, Houston, TX, 77002, United States

We want HOU! Join us to discover NASA’s toughest tech challenges–and apply your skills and expertise to solve them.In these presentations, occurring the fourth Thursdays of the month, Mr. Montgomery Goforth and other aerospace subject matter experts will discuss the technology development challenges faced by NASA’s Johnson Space Center and the surrounding Aerospace community in our ongoing efforts as the hub of human spaceflight. Presentations will focus on the ways in which these challenges, and the associated opportunities, can be leveraged by Houston’s innovation community.Join us after the talk for drinks and networking at Second Draught!Past NASA Tech Talks have included: Montgomery B. Goforth discussing NASA Intellectual Property suitable for commercialization by small/start-up companies; Jared Daum discussing Orion’s parachutes and risk identification and mitigation that are showstoppers for the spaceflight parachute industry; Kevin Kobylka discussing current Guidance, Navigation, and Control (GNC) capabilities and needs; and Nick Skytland, Chief Technologist at the Johnson Space Center (JSC) and Director of the Business Development and Technology Integration Office. Future NASA Tech Talks will include: dual-use (space/terrestrial) technologies in the areas of: Avionics / Communication / Sensors Radio Frequency Identification (RFID) technology for ultra-long lived wireless sensors Power Storage and Distribution Autonomous & Robotics Systems Extra-Vehicular Activity (EVA) Systems Virtual/Augmented Reality in Human Spaceflight Training & Execution Human Health & Performance / Human Systems Research About the Speakers Montgomery B. Goforth has more than 40 years of experience as both Engineer and Manager in a variety of highly technical space and defense-related efforts.  After working for a small defense contractor specializing in anti-submarine warfare, he joined NASA’s Johnson Space Center in 1990.  He has held leadership positions in multiple JSC Directorates and Program Offices. In his current role as Assistant Director of the JSC Engineering Directorate, he has established a framework to better focus Engineering’s efforts towards Human Spaceflight technology and systems development consistent with JSC and Agency goals for exploration. He has used this framework to guide Engineering’s internal investments and external partnerships supporting Lunar and Mars Exploration. Mr. Goforth was born in East Texas, raised in Southern California and Arizona, and graduated from Rice University (BSME 1982, MSME 1987). NASA Tech Talks topics include dual-use (space/terrestrial) technologies and focus areas in: Avionics / Communication / Sensors Radio Frequency Identification (RFID) technology for ultra-long-lived wireless sensors Power Storage and Distribution Autonomous & Robotics Systems Extra-Vehicular Activity (EVA) Systems Virtual/Augmented Reality in Human Spaceflight Training & Execution Human Health & Performance / Human Systems Research NASA Intellectual Property suitable for commercialization by small/start-up companies Orion’s parachutes and risk identification and mitigation strategies Leveraging Radio Frequency Identification (RFID) technology for ultra-long-lived wireless sensors

From Ion District

item.title: Houston World of Hot Wheels IV
item.description:
Saturday, July 25, 2026 at 10 AM

🚗🏁 Houston World of Hot Wheels IV is roaring back with more thrills and speed! 🏁🚗 After an electric, action-packed weekend of nonstop deals, racing, and collector excitement, Houston World of Hot Wheels IV is revving up for another round! We’re bringing even MORE vendors, MORE cars, and MORE action for collectors, racers, and families to enjoy all weekend long. ⚡   📅 July 25–26, 2026 | 10AM–5PM 📍 9700 Bissonnet St 1500, Houston, TX 🎟️ FREE Entry + FREE Parking   Event Highlights 🛒 Hot Wheels Swap & Shop – Both Days 50+ vendors. 30,000+ Hot Wheels, Matchbox, and diecast to dig through. STHs, TH variations, premium Car Culture, redline-era pieces, MOC vintage, loose mainline bins, M2s, Greenlights, Johnny Lightnings. Bring your want list!   🚗✨ Houston Diecast Custom Contest – 10AM-3PM | Both Days Calling all builders and custom kings 👑 Bring your best custom Hot Wheels/Matchbox build — paint, wheel swaps, stance, body kits, full one-of-one creations. This contest is for the detail freaks and the true “trust the process” customizers. 🏆 The winner will be announced at 4PM and will receive a prize sponsored by Domingo! ✨ Hosted by @funopolistoys   🏁 Hot Wheels Racing Contest – Both Days The stage track is live all weekend. Hourly races, prizes for winners, FREE to enter.   🏎️ Car Show (Parking Lot Showcase) Take it outside and check out Houston’s coolest real-life rides. Style, horsepower, and car-community energy all in one spot.   👶 Mini Zones & Family Fun Fun spaces for younger collectors and first‑time racers — all ages welcome!   🚨 VENDORS WANTED Got STHs, vintage redlines, custom builds, or bulk mainline to move? This is your crowd. 📥 Apply: https://tinyurl.com/CCSGuestVendorFromHouston   🏨 Special Hotel Deals Enjoy a special room rate of $79/night at TownePlace Suites, just about 15 minutes from the venue! 👉 Book: https://www.marriott.com/event-reservations/reservation-link.mi?id=1767823967549&key=CORP&app=resvlink   Hunt or race. Trade or just walk the floor. The customs will be parked outside, the bins will be open inside, and the track will be running all weekend. See you July 25–26.

Know your why, and we will get to the future together. The future is only a cup away. Register Here: https://www.cupofjoey.org/

From Ion District

item.description:
Friday, July 31, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Ion, 4201 Main Street, Houston, TX, 77002, United States

About SCORE Office Hours SCORE is the nation’s largest network of volunteer, expert business mentors. Since 1964, SCORE has helped more than 11 million entrepreneurs start, grow, or successfully exit a business. SCORE’s 10,000 volunteers provide free, expert mentoring, resources, and education in all 50 U.S. states and territories. Office Hours with SCORE take place each Friday morning. Additional partners will be on-site the second Friday of each month (excluding holidays)

item.description:
Friday, July 31, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Ion, 4201 Main Street, Houston, TX, 77002, United States

NASA is bringing its expertise to Houston’s innovation hub! Every other Friday, join us for NASA’s office hours, designed to bridge the gap between Houston’s innovators, entrepreneurs, and startups with NASA’s elite experts, capabilities, and technology.  License the Future: Discover how to utilize NASA-developed technologies and intellectual property for your own projects. Partner for Growth: Explore how to grow your own goals and NASA’s mission together by leveraging unique human spaceflight capabilities and expertise from NASA Johnson Space Center. item.title: Mayor's Back 2 School and Health Fair
item.description:
Saturday, August 1, 2026 at 12 AM

Location: George R Brown Convention Center, 1001 Avenida de las Americas, Houston, TX, 77010, United States

When:August 1, 2026 What:The Annual Mayor’s Back 2 School and Health Fair presented by Shell provides 20,000 Houston area students with the tools they need to start the school year off right. In addition to distributing 20,000 backpacks with school supplies, the event will feature on-site health screenings to include: dental screenings and sealants, vision screenings, mental health screenings, and routine immunizations. The event also connects families to a wide range of health and social services offered by community agencies. Where:George R. Brown Convention Center

From Houston Food Bank

Event details

item.title: Houston Restaurant Weeks 2026
item.description:
Saturday, August 1, 2026 at 12 AM

Houston Restaurant Weeks is the largest annual fundraiser for America’s largest food bank, the Houston Food Bank. It’s a month-long dining extravaganza featuring specially priced lunches, brunches, and dinners at restaurants from The Woodlands to Galveston. Participating restaurants make donations based on how many prix fixe brunches, lunches, and dinners they sell during the event. Diners are not solicited for donations. Beginning July 15, diners can visit HoustonRestaurantWeeks.com to view the 2026 list of restaurants that have committed to participate, along with their HRW menus. The event includes restaurants located in Harris, Galveston, and Montgomery counties. The Galveston County Food Bank and Montgomery County Food Bank are part of the Houston Food Bank’s Affiliate Food Banks. The Houston Food Bank provides food to these smaller food banks, which in turn distribute the food to hunger relief charities in a portion of HFB’s service area. The Houston Food Bank certifies that their Affiliate Food Banks meet Feeding America standards.

From Houston Food Bank

item.title: CCS $20,000 cEDH Invitational Qualifier #3
item.description:
Saturday, August 1, 2026 at 9 AM

🏆 CCS $20,000 cEDH Invitational Qualifier #3 🏆 The race tightens. The margins shrink. Qualifier #3 brings us into the mid season of the CCS $50,000 cEDH Invitational Series. With only three events after, every decision matters and every point is gold. Full Details → https://topdeck.gg/event/ccs-50000-cedh-invitational   🗓 Dates: August 1–2, 2026 📍 Location: Charlie’s Collectible Show, 9700 Bissonett St Unit 1500, Houston, TX 77036 Tournament Details: 🎟️ Entry Fee: $90 before July 18, $110 After 🔗 Register Now! 👉  https://topdeck.gg/event/css-20000-cedh-invitational-qualifier-4 🗂️ Format & Structure Format: cEDH (Proxy Friendly) Saturday: 6 Swiss Rounds (based on attendance) Sunday: 2 Swiss Rounds → Top 16 🚫 Proxy Requirements: Full card name, mana cost, type line, and effect text Test cards or clearly written MDFCs allowed No paper slip-ins permitted 💰 Guaranteed $20,000 Prize Pool 1st — $5,000 + Auto Invite + TopDeck Invite + 200 pts 2nd-4th — $2,000 each + Auto Invite + 160 pts 5th–8th — $550 + 120 pts 9th–16th — $550 + 100 pts 17th–32nd — $175 + 75 pts 33rd–48th — 60 pts 49th–64th — 45 pts 65th–96th — 30 pts 97th–128th — 20 pts Event Schedule: 🕔 Registration Opens: 9 AM 🕔 Player Meeting (Day 1): 10 AM 🕔 Day 2 Start Time: 9 AM 🛍️ Collectible Marketplace Between rounds, enjoy access to a curated marketplace featuring: TCG singles, sealed, and accessories Retro video games & consoles Anime merch, figures, and models Toys and other hobby collectibles Perfect for grabbing upgrades, browsing grails, or just hanging out between matches. 🏨 Special Hotel Deals Enjoy a special rate of $79/night — 2 Queens at TownePlace Suites, only 15 minutes distance from the action! Book your stay 👉🏻 https://www.marriott.com/event-reservations/reservation-link.mi?id=1767823967549&key=CORP&app=resvlink 🔔 IMPORTANT REMINDERS Player names will appear as real names on published documents. Top 16 decklists will be published. 🔥 Late-season stakes hit hard — tighten your game and push forward. Your run to the $50K Invitational continues at CCS cEDH Qualifier #3!

From CCS Houston

Event details

item.title: Houston Comic Exchange II
item.description:
Saturday, August 1, 2026 at 10 AM

Houston Comic Exchange II – Built for Comic Collectors Summer is prime comic-hunting season, and CCS Houston Comic Exchange II brings together collectors, dealers, and fans for a day of buying, selling, trading, and discovering comics. From key issues and first appearances to hidden gems and box-fresh finds, every table offers something worth digging for. No gatekeeping, no pressure, just comics and the people who love them.   📅 Saturday, August 1, 2026 | 10AM – 5PM 📍 9700 Bissonnet St, Unit 1500, Houston, TX 🎟️ FREE Entry + FREE Parking   🔥 Event Highlights 🔥 📦 50 Vendor Tables – All Comics, All Formats Singles, back issues, key issues, first appearances, graded slabs, raw runs, indie and small press, variant covers. Vendor tables dedicated entirely to comics, no toys, no filler. If you’re a comic collector, this floor was built for you.   🔍 Key Issues and First Appearances This is where the hunt lives. Vendors bring the deep cuts, raw back issues worth digging through, graded books if you’re chasing condition, and the kind of keys that actually show up when serious collectors are clearing their own collections to make room.   📚 Back-Issue Digging and Indie Discovery Not every great find is a grail. Long boxes of back issues across decades and publishers, plus indie comics and small press titles that never make it to a standard shop shelf. Come with a list or come ready to browse – both work.   🤝 Vendors Who Collect Too The people behind these tables are the same people who will ask what you’re hunting before they start pulling books for you. The Houston Comic Exchange is a community floor first. The conversations are part of the event.   🛒 Vendor Table Applications Houston comic vendors: we’re gathering a focused comics-only crowd, and free entry is driving serious foot traffic. Apply for your table here 👉🏻 https://forms.gle/7q6pjcoiJTPbJouP8   The key issue you’ve been tracking is probably in one of those long boxes this summer. The Houston Comic Exchange II, August 1. Free entry, free parking, always.

From CCS Houston

item.title: Houston Toy Exchange III
item.description:
Sunday, August 2, 2026 at 5 AM

Houston Toy Exchange III — The Floor Is Loaded, Houston Somewhere on this floor is the piece you have been hunting for months. 🔥 Houston Toy Exchange III is a pure toy marketplace, no convention noise, no filler. Just collectors, families, and dealers moving action figures, vintage finds, and modern grails across one packed floor at CCS Houston. The fun is in the hunt: digging through a bin you have never seen, talking shop with someone who knows the line better than you do, and walking out with something you did not expect to find.   📅 Date: Sunday, August 2, 2026 | 10AM – 5PM 📍 Location: 9700 Bissonnet St, #1500, Houston, TX 77036 🎟️ FREE Entry + FREE Parking   🔥 What You’ll Find on the Floor 🔥 🛍️ Toy Marketplace Tables packed with action figures, vintage toys, sealed and loose, nostalgia pieces, and the modern lines collectors are actually chasing right now. Every table is its own dig. Come search the floor.   💰 Buy, Sell, Trade Bring the toys you are ready to pass along or trade up for something you have been chasing. Collectors and vendors are buying, selling, and trading all day. Connect, negotiate, and keep great pieces moving through the community.   🔍 Toy Appraisals Holding something and not sure what it’s worth? One of our experienced vendors is running toy appraisals all day, sharing insights on value, rarity, and trade potential so you can make confident, informed deals on the floor.   🧩 Toy Scavenger Hunt Challenge Turn the hunt into a game. Work the marketplace and complete the Toy Hunt Challenge by tracking down specific toy categories across multiple booths for a shot at prizes and collector bragging rights.   📸 Toy Flex Wall Score something special? Show it off. Snap a photo of your best pickup at the Toy Flex Wall and share your find with the community.   Longtime collector or just starting out, Houston Toy Exchange III is about connecting, swapping stories, and keeping the love of toys alive together. Bring your toys, bring your curiosity, pull up.   Want in as a vendor? Tables are open: https://tinyurl.com/CCSGuestVendorFromHouston   Houston Toy Exchange III. Bring your toys, bring your curiosity, and come move some pieces.

From CCS Houston

item.title: Houston TCG Con X
item.description:
Friday, August 7, 2026 at 10 AM

🃏 Houston TCG Con X | Every Format. One Floor. Three Days. 🃏 Three days. Every format in the room. The Houston TCG community takes over CCS August 7–9 for Pokémon, MTG, One Piece, Lorcana, and Yu-Gi-Oh. Tournament grinders and binder flippers on the same floor, doing what this community does best.   📅 Date: Friday–Sunday, August 7–9, 2026 ⏰ Time: Friday: 3PM–10PM | Saturday: 10AM–6PM | Sunday: 10AM–5PM 📍 Location: 9700 Bissonnet St, Unit 1500, Houston, TX 🎟️ FREE Entry + FREE Parking   🔥 Event Highlights 🔥 🃔 Multi-Game Vendor Floor Pokémon. MTG. One Piece. Lorcana. Yu-Gi-Oh. Singles vendors, sealed product, and binder traders across every major format. Three full days to hunt, flip, and deal face-to-face.   🏆 Multi-Game Tournaments Competitive rounds running all weekend across formats. Tournament schedule and registration details coming soon. Follow CCS Houston for updates.   📦 Full Marketplace Sports cards, anime merch, figures, comics, and toys rounding out the vendor floor for everything you collect beyond the card game.   💼 Vendor Tables Available TCG dealers, singles vendors, sports card sellers, anime and collectible booths. Houston’s biggest TCG weekend is yours. Apply: https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSdei0apYj2qU4fQIiz9rrqVDHgqmFZSpLRzYDAufcg3B8BaXA/viewform   🏨 Traveling to Houston? Book before it fills: https://www.marriott.com/event-reservations/reservation-link.mi?id=1767823967549&key=CORP&app=resvlink Three days, every format, free entry. See you in August, Houston.

item.description:

🏆 Legacy Duelist Series Houston🏆 Charlie’s Collectible Show is proud to partner with Yggdrasil Materias to bring the Legacy Duelist Series to Houston! If you’re a duelist who loves retro formats, then this weekend is a must. LDS Houston features an Edison main event, an Edison 3v3 Sunday Event, and a Goat format Sunday tournament. Yggdrasil Materias will be slinging supplies, and CCS will be providing the Yu-Gi-Oh! Singles.   🗓 Dates: August 8-9, 2026 📍 Location: Charlie’s Collectible Show, 9700 Bissonett St Unit 1500, Houston, TX 77036   Tournament Details: 🎟️ Main Event Entry Fee: $60 (SOLD OUT) 🎟️ Day 2 Edison 3v3 Entry Fee: $115 🎟️ Day 2 Goat Entry Fee: $40 🔗 Main Event Page 👉  https://www.yggdrasilmaterias.com/product/lds-houston-ygo-tournament-day-1-edison-format 🔗 Register For Sunday Edison 3v3 Now! 👉  https://www.yggdrasilmaterias.com/product/lds-houston-ygo-tournament-day-2-3v3-edison 🔗 Register For Sunday Goat Format Now! 👉 https://www.yggdrasilmaterias.com/product/lds-houston-ygo-tournament-day-2-goat   🗂️ Format & Structure Formats: Day 1: Edison Main Event | Day 2: Main Event Top Cut, Edison 3v3 & Goat Format Day 1 — Edison Tournament: 9 rounds of Swiss Day 2 — Edison Tournament Top Cut Day 2 — Edison 3v3 Tournament 6 rounds of Swiss with cut to Top 8 Day 2 — Goat Tournament: 7 rounds of Swiss with cut to Top 8   💰 Edison Format Prizing: 1st: SJCS Dark End Dragon + First Place Playmat + LDS Champs Invite 2nd: Ultimate Rare Caius The Shadow Monarch x2 + Top 8 Playmat + LDS Champs Invite 3rd: Ultimate Rare Caius The Shadow Monarch + Top 8 Playmat + LDS Champs Invite 4th: Ultimate Rare Dark Armed Dragon x1 + Top 8 Playmat + LDS Champs Invite 5th – 8th: Ultimate Rare Judgment Dragon x1 + Top 8 Playmat + LDS Champs Invite 9th -16th: Colossal Fighter Ultimate Rare x1 + Top 16 Playmat 17th-32nd: Tragoedia Ultimate Rare x1 + Top 32 Playmat   💰 Edison 3v3 Prizing: 1st Place Team: Ultimate Rare Dark Armed Dragon x3 + Top Cut Playmat x3 2nd Place Team: Champion Pack Trap Dustshoot x3 + Top Cut Playmat x3 3rd-4th Place Team: TBD 5th-8th Place Team: TBD   💰 Goat Format Prizing: 1st: TP2 Morphing Jar + First Place Playmat 2nd: Champion Pack Book of Moon + Top Cut Playmat 3rd-4th: TP7 Breaker the Magical Warrior + Top Cut Playmat 5th – 8th: Ultimate Rare Tsukuyomi + Top Cut Playmat   Day 1 Event Schedule: 🕔 Doors Open: 7:00 AM 🕔 Check-In Cutoff: 8:30 AM 🕔 Start Time: 8:45 AM   Day 2 Event Schedule: 🕔 Doors Open: 7:00 AM 🕔 Main Event Top Cut Start: 7:30 AM 🕔 Goat Check-In Cutoff: 7:45 AM 🕔 Goat Tournament Start: 8:00 AM 🕔 Edison 3v3 Check-In Cutoff: 8:45 AM 🕔 Edison 3v3 Tournament Start 9:00 AM

item.title: Retro Video Game Expo III
item.description:
Saturday, August 15, 2026 at 10 AM

🕹️ Houston, Press Start on a Weekend of Pure Retro Nostalgia! 👾 Step back into the golden era of gaming at Retro Video Game Expo III, a two-day celebration of classic consoles, arcade legends, competitive retro tournaments, and nonstop free play. From expanded free-play zones spanning the 80s and beyond to live, high-energy tournament matchups and a collector-focused marketplace, this expo is built for gamers who chase high scores and grail finds.   🗓️ Date & Time: August 15–16, 2026 | 10AM–5PM 📍 Location: 9700 Bissonnet St #1500, Houston, TX 77036 💥 FREE ENTRY. FREE PARKING. FREE PLAY. 🎮 Event Highlights 🎮 Retro Video Game Marketplace – All Weekend Step into a collector’s paradise. Vendors from across the region are bringing vintage consoles, original cartridges, imports, manuals, accessories, and hard-to-find titles. This is where treasures respawn. 🚨 Want to vend? Sign up here 👉 https://tinyurl.com/HoustonVendors   🎮 Free-Play Zone – ALL Day Jump on 80’s Arcades & Classic Consoles and relive the games that made gaming legendary. More setups and activities to be announced soon! 🤝 Calling All Collaborators & Retro Communities This event is built with the community for community! We’re actively looking to partner with: 🏁 Tournament organizers → https://forms.gle/74Qvuv9PFzeDVF1V8 🕹️ Pinball/Arcade Hosts → https://forms.gle/74Qvuv9PFzeDVF1V8 🎤 Workshops & Panels → https://forms.gle/q2nCKsZ6FEVZBgqW9 🙌 Volunteer Sign-Up → https://forms.gle/15zMGTqZPSGKjGdy6 🎮 Vendor Applications → https://tinyurl.com/CCSGuestVendorFromHouston If you’ve ever wanted to be part of event, bring your passion! Let’s chat! 📩   🏨 Special Hotel Deals Enjoy a special rate of $79/night — 2 Queens at TownePlace Suites, only 15 minutes distance from the action! Book your stay 👉🏻 https://www.marriott.com/event-reservations/reservation-link.mi?id=1767823967549&key=CORP&app=resvlink   Bring your crew, grab a controller, and let’s relive the golden age of gaming together. 👾✨

From CCS Houston

item.title: Author Talk with Dr. Pamela Toler, Author of The Dragon From Chicago
item.description:
Thursday, August 27, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: Albert and Ethel Herzstein Theater on the Mady and Ken Kades Stage

Join Holocaust Museum Houston for a night with Dr. Pamela Toler, Author of The Dragon from Chicago: The Untold Story of an American Reporter in Nazi Germany.      “Today we face an alarming upsurge in the spread of misinformation and attempts by powerful figures to discredit facts so they can seize control of our political and cultural narratives. These are threats American journalist Sigrid Schultz knew all too well. The Chicago Tribune’s Berlin bureau chief and primary foreign correspondent for Central Europe from 1925 to January 1941, Schultz witnessed Hitler’s rise to power and was one of the first reporters—male or female—to warn American readers of the growing dangers of Nazism.  She was one of the last to leave Berlin before it was too late.     In The Dragon From Chicago, historian Pamela Toler tells the story of Schultz’s years spent courageously reporting the news from Berlin, from the revolts of 1919 through the Nazi rise to power and Allied air raids over Berlin in 1941. At a time when women reporters rarely wrote front-page stories, Schultz regularly  reported the truth about Nazi Germany in the face of censorship and the threat of expulsion, internment, or death. The Nazis called Schultz “that dragon from Chicago.” One of her fellow correspondents called her “Adolph Hitler’s greatest enemy.” Schultz herself claimed to be “just a reporter.” Her story is a powerful account of one woman standing up for truth in an era marked by the spread of disinformation and propaganda spawned by hate.”     Books will be available for purchase by credit card.

From Holocaust Museum Houston

item.title: CCS Card Show I
item.description:
Saturday, August 29, 2026 at 10 AM

CCS Card Show I — Every Sport. Every TCG. One Floor. Houston, the hobby comes home. For the first time at CCS, every major sport and every major TCG land under one roof: sports card collectors, memorabilia hunters, and TCG players moving cards on the same floor, the way it should be. Modern hits to vintage grails, sealed wax to graded slabs, raw singles to game-used pieces. Buy, sell, trade, grade. This is the room.   📅 Date: August 29-30, 2026 | 10AM – 5PM 📍 Location: 9700 Bissonnet St, Unit 1500, Houston, TX 77036 🎟️ FREE Entry + FREE Parking   🔥 Event Highlights 🔥 ⚾ The Ultimate Sports Card & Memorabilia Marketplace Explore vendors dedicated to sports cards, high-end memorabilia, and rare collectibles from baseball, football, basketball, and more. If you’re hunting for a signed rookie card, vintage set, or exclusive autograph, this is THE place to level up your collection.   🃏 Trading Card Game Extravaganza 🃏 We’re also featuring top TCG vendors offering a treasure trove of Pokémon, Yu-Gi-Oh!, Magic: The Gathering, One Piece, Lorcana, and more! Chase that elusive rare pull or build your deck for your next tournament, you’ll find the best deals on booster packs, graded singles, sealed products, and Japanese imports.   💼 Vendors Wanted Got a table’s worth to move? Guest vendors are welcome at Card Show I. The person across the table is a fan like you. Claim your spot 👉🏻 https://forms.gle/sZdrRxHkEXM69kbN6   🏨 Traveling to Houston? Lock In Your Rate Enjoy a special rate of $79/night — ****2 Queens at TownePlace Suites, only 15 minutes away from the action! 👉 https://www.marriott.com/event-reservations/reservation-link.mi?id=1767823967549&key=CORP&app=resvlink   Houston’s first CCS Card Show. Every sport, every TCG, one floor. Pull up.

From CCS Houston

item.title: Unified Topgolf Pro-Am (Houston-Katy)
item.description:
Friday, September 18, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Topgolf-Katy, 1030 Memorial Brook Blvd, Houston, TX 77084, Houston, 77084

The Unified Topgolf Pro-Am is one of the most exciting and meaningful community partnership events hosted by Special Olympics Texas, bringing together athletes, families, coaches, volunteers, corporate partners, and local business leaders for an unforgettable day of golf, laughter, connection, and community impact. Each bay includes a Special Olympics Texas athlete paired alongside five golfers, creating a truly unique experience where relationships, memories, and meaningful moments are built throughout the day. From friendly competition and team spirit to incredible conversations and shared experiences, the event is designed to bring people together in a fun and welcoming environment while directly supporting local athletes and programs. Proceeds from the Unified Topgolf Pro-Am help provide year-round sports training, competitions, health initiatives, leadership development, and educational opportunities for children, teenagers, and adults with intellectual disabilities throughout the greater Houston area. As the event continues growing each year, the Unified Topgolf Pro-Am has quickly become a tremendous opportunity for organizations and community members to make a real impact while being part of something incredibly energetic, uplifting, and memorable. For more information regarding sponsorship opportunities, team entries, volunteer involvement, or sponsorship opportunities, please reach out to Jared Kossover, Strategic Partnerships Manager with Special Olympics Texas Jared Kossover E: jkossover@sotx.org C: 737-867-9659

From Energy Corridor Farmers Market

item.title: Demon Slayer Mini Con
item.description:
Saturday, September 19, 2026 at 10 AM

🔥 Calling all Demon Slayer Corps members, Hashira-in-training, and Tanjiro fans**!** 🔥 From Mizunoto beginners to seasoned Hashira, masters of Sun Breathing to the loyal members of the Zenitsu-squad, Demon Slayer Mini Con is your next mission! CCS Houston is hosting a one-day Total Concentration event for every slayer who lives for the Hinokami Kagura, the intensity of the Upper Rank battles, and the bond between friends. Join the Corps for a day packed with Nichirin-grade marketplace finds, Cosplay haoris, and Blood Demon Art energy, all in a fan-first space where no one is left in the Final Selection alone. Steel your heart, grab your Kasugai Crow, and let’s set our hearts ablaze together ⚔️✨   🗓️ Date & Time: Saturday, Sept 19, 2026 | 10AM – 5PM 📍 Location: 9700 Bissonnet St, #1500, Houston, TX 77036 🎟️ FREE Entry + FREE Parking   👘 Demon Slayer Cosplay Showcase + Demon Face-Off (2 PM) Prepare for a Hashira-level showdown in a dramatic pose-off against our “demon.” The crowd’s roar will determine who survives the night and claims victory.   🎴 Nichirin Sword Pose-Off (3PM) Even without a uniform, your spirit can burn bright! Grab a Nichirin blade, take the stage, and unleash your most iconic Breathing Style pose for a chance to win a prize.   🎮 Demon Slayer: Hinokami Chronicles Free-Play Zone (All Day) Jump into the action in our gaming area! Join free play sessions on the Demon Slayer: The Hinokami Chronicles video game and challenge fellow slayers to a dueling trial.   Demon Slayer Trivia (3:30 PM) Only those with the sharpest focus will survive this mental trial and earn their rank as a true scholar of the series.   🎨 Nezuko’s Box DIY Craft Station (All Day) Channel your inner Urokodaki to construct a miniature wooden-style carrier using paper, charms, and markers. Open to all ages!   🔥 Demon Slayer Artist Alley Wander through a marketplace filled with hand-forged treasures and fandom collectibles, including custom Nichirin charms, Demon Slayer-inspired prints, and talismans for the next mission, our Artist Alley features the most talented creators in the Corps. 🔥 Want to create something special for Demon Slayer fans? 🔗 Host activities, workshops, or panels → https://forms.gle/q2nCKsZ6FEVZBgqW9 🔗 Volunteer Sign-Up → https://forms.gle/15zMGTqZPSGKjGdy6 🔗 Vendor Applications → https://tinyurl.com/HoustonVendors

From CCS Houston

Event details

item.title: SUP & Kayak Social
item.description:
Saturday, September 19, 2026 at 8 AM

[water-sport] Cool off with a little SUP action! We'll bring Stand Up Paddleboards to share. If you’ve never SUP’ed before, we’ll show you the basics strokes and how to stand on the board. Plus the proper way to hold the paddle!

item.title: Community Conversation Volunteer Shift
item.description:
Wednesday, September 23, 2026 at 8 AM

Location: Houston Food Bank East Branch, 535 Portwall Street, Houston, TX, 77029, United States

Join a truly unique volunteer shift where neighbors, community leaders, and food bank staff come together to share experiences and bridge mutual understanding. The gathering is highly interactive and uses structured conversations to build stronger and more connected communities. Whether you join individually or as a group, this experience moves beyond food distribution to focus on lasting community impact! Register Here

From Houston Food Bank
